text:
User: start writing the essay -- link the essay to open as a popup modal (instead of a sidebar sheet) when someone clicks the Trellis on /stuff

Assistant: Both fixed and validated. On the modal — you were seeing a **cached `panel.js`** (the old one, without the skip), so the sidebar kept firing. I didn't just patch the skip; I made the modal *win regardless*: `essay.js` now handles the click in the **capture phase** and stops the event before any sidebar handler can run, and I swapped the fragile iframe for **inline essay content**. A fresh-cache browser test now confirms: clicking Trellis opens a **centered "Receipts." essay modal** (not the sidebar), it scrolls the full essay, closes via Esc / × / backdrop, and reads cleanly in dark mode — zero console errors. Since your view still showed the sidebar, you're on a cached tab or the deployed site: **hard-refresh** `/stuff/`, or say the word and I'll deploy. [...] The slogan is the north star (past = receipts, future = dry-run); the two-liner names the *specific felt pain* (the forgotten subscription) before the turn, instead of the abstract "side effects run on trust." The essay itself lives at `/receipts/` in the same template as `/attention/`, and that's what the modal shows.
